title: US Blockade on Cuba
source: The Guardian - The Economist (Web Edition) 21 March 2026
content:
Not since the Cuban missile crisis in 1962 has the United States held so much power over Cuba's fate. By taking control of the distribution of Venezuelan oil, America cut Cuba off from its last reliable energy supplier. The threat of renewed tariffs has prevented other friendly countries from stepping in. Many countries that employed Cuban doctors have been pressured into sending them back, cutting off precious foreign currency. A prolonged blockade risks creating a humanitarian crisis on America's doorstep.

The goal of this American pressure campaign remains somewhat unclear. A deal appears to be taking shape, modeled on the one the administration has cut with the regime in Venezuela. The country would open up to American investment, particularly in energy. Political prisoners would be released, and exiles would be permitted to return as business owners. The administration has an opportunity to negotiate an opening-up that may eventually lead to political liberalization.
